reasoning: FIDE releases its official ranking list on the 1st of every month, which is then updated on Wikipedia. Between November 9 and November 16, no new official standard rating list will be published, meaning her Wikipedia ranking will remain completely unchanged at #19 with near absolute certainty (prob = 0.98). For the December 9 and February 7 resolution dates, the probability depends on the December 2025, January 2026, and February 2026 lists. Dronavalli's rating has slightly fallen over the last few months (from 2491 in July to 2466 in November). If she is inactive in late 2025/early 2026, there is a significant risk that players rated just below her (e.g., Nino Batsiashvili at #20 with 2461) might have good performances and overtake her, slowly drifting her rank down. If she does play, her Elo rating is self-adjusting, offering roughly a coin-flip chance of improving or worsening. Given the general slight downward pressure from inactivity and rising stars like Divya Deshmukh passing her recently, probabilities for maintaining or improving on #19 are estimated slightly below 50% for the subsequent months, decreasing further with time due to cumulative drift.
